search_queries_used: site:cpoc.org.uk surgery opioids chronic pain preoperative assessment
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic management or dose instructions for Chronic pain pre-op documentation
generic anesthesia landing pages without an exact section for Chronic pain pre-op documentation
rejection_reasons: Dose conversion and taper instructions were outside scope and unsafe without specialist review.
The candidate source category "automatic taper schedules" did not provide exact documentation-only evidence for this workflow and was not mapped.
Autonomous management, medication, dose, procedure, escalation, or discharge instructions were outside the documentation-only scope.
Landing pages and search snippets were not accepted as exact evidence.
population_applicability: Surgical patients with chronic pain or preoperative opioid use.
setting_applicability: Direct for preoperative history and documentation; management requires the perioperative and pain team.
UAE_applicability: International CPOC guidance; UAE controlled-medicine and perioperative pathways require local review.
recency_verification: Official CPOC 2021 PDF was reviewed on 2026-07-13; its scheduled 2024 review is overdue.
superseded_check: No newer CPOC surgery-and-opioids document was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact CPOC section supports recording chronic pain, functional impact, current opioid use, psychosocial or substance context, and specialist involvement before surgery.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting chronic pain, opioid use, medicine history, psychosocial context, alcohol or illicit substance context, function, and specialist involvement.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Chronic pain pre-op documentation; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

unresolved_source_gaps: Pain diagnosis, opioid equivalence, dose, tapering, withdrawal, analgesic plan, referral, and controlled-drug governance remain unsupported.
Legacy duration presets, broad generic interval-history wording, and any content not explicitly mapped to an exact section remain unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No source mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, anesthesia plan, investigation, procedure, medication, dose, referral, discharge decision, or urgency conclusion.
UAE facility policy, credentialing, pathway, and qualified anesthesiologist review remain required before use.

legacy_item_support_mappings.direct_relationship: The exact CPOC section supports recording chronic pain, functional impact, current opioid use, psychosocial or substance context, and specialist involvement before surgery.
